sections.heading: Problem Statement
sections.content: Agents working across multiple conversations need a shared record of what has been verified, when, and by whom. Without this audit trail, each new agent session starts blind — it cannot tell which nodes have already been reviewed, whether a prior verification is still valid, or how long a node has been stale. The history subsystem provides this audit trail and is the primary data source for the impact report. In V1, history is infrastructure: the data is collected automatically and queried by `cortex report`. Interactive CLI browsing is deferred to V2.

sections.heading: User Stories
sections.content:
**US-HI-01 — Automatic change recording**
As an agent, I want every code change to automatically record a history row so that I can see how long a node has been stale and what changed without any manual tracking.

**US-HI-02 — Verification audit trail**
As an agent, I want to see which nodes were previously verified, by whom, and with what reason so that I don't re-verify work that a prior agent already reviewed.

**US-HI-03 — History as impact report source**
As an agent, I want the history table to be the source for the impact report so that the report reflects the actual change record without any additional aggregation layer.

sections.heading: Current Capabilities
sections.content:
| Capability | Status |
|---|---|
| `node_history` auto-recording on every node upsert | Done |
| `INITIAL` change type (first discovery) | Done |
| `CODE_ONLY`, `DESC_ONLY`, `CODE_AND_DESC` change types | Done |
| `AGENT_VERIFIED`, `MANUAL_VERIFIED` change types | Done |
| `CHECKPOINT` change type | Done |
| `preserved=1` for verification rows (never auto-pruned) | Done |
| `_HISTORY_ROW_LIMIT = 100` hard cap per node | Done |
| `cortex history checkpoint` command | Done |
| `cortex history agent-verified` command | Done |
| `cortex_history` MCP tool (agent access) | Done |
| `cortex report` (impact report from history) | Not started |
| `cortex history` CLI browser (interactive) | Not started |

sections.heading: Backlog
sections.content:
**`cortex report`** — The V1 deliverable that makes history useful to the verification gate. Queries `node_history` for all changes since the last checkpoint, joins with `node_verification` for current verification status (verified_by, reason, timestamp), and `test_results` for the latest test outcome per changed node. Also includes CONTENT_STALE doc sections. Outputs structured markdown suitable for attaching to a PR. Pure SQL read — deterministic, no LLM.

**`cortex history` CLI browser** — An interactive command to browse per-node history with stale-window annotations ("stale for N days since CODE_ONLY at T"). The data is already recorded in V1; the UX is deferred. The `cortex_history` MCP tool already provides this for agent access.

**Checkpoint pruning UX** — `cortex history checkpoint` exists but is not documented as part of the everyday workflow. Needs clearer guidance on when to run it (pre-release, post-merge) and what `--keep` and `--older-than` values are appropriate for typical projects.

**VCS change IDs in history rows** — Store the jj/git change ID alongside history rows so reports can include the commit description as context for why a node went stale. The `git_sha` column exists in the schema but is not populated during normal builds. Requires a design decision on whether to use git SHA, jj change ID, or both.
